source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - MC 941 Platform A Hydrocarbon Release

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 941

actions_taken: Gas detector ASH 0500 activated alarm at 0933 hours
Production Supervisor and Day Lead Operator assessed leak area at 0936 hours
Workers showered and reported to sickbay for medical evaluation
Workers cleared by medic and physician with no injuries
Equinor implemented engineering controls for residual fluids and static pressure release
Review of isolation procedures to identify dog-legs and dead-legs
event.summary: On 10 May 2023 at approximately 0930 hours, two workers employed by Grand Isle Shipyard were removing bolts from a flange on a 2-inch Well Equalization Header line during a scheduled shut-in at MC 941 Platform A (Titan). As the workers loosened the last bolt, trapped pressure within the line escaped and sprayed the two workers in the face with a small amount of hydrocarbon fluid. The release activated a nearby gas detector, sounding the platform alarm. No injuries, pollution, or equipment damage occurred. The incident was caused by oversight of residual fluids and trapped pressure in a dog-leg bend above the flange during the isolation process.
recommendations: Review isolations to look for any possibility of hydrocarbon release from dog-legs or dead-legs due to residual fluid static pressure remaining after lockout/tagout is completed, even if zero pressure is indicated on the gauge
Implement engineering controls for residual fluids or static pressure release
Ensure personnel review isolations for potential hydrocarbon release from dog-legs or dead-legs even if gauge indicates zero pressure

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon fluid in isolated lines
Residual fluids in dead-legs and dog-leg bends

threats.name: Failure to identify and account for residual pressure in dog-leg bend during isolation
Incomplete isolation procedure overlooking dead-leg sections

consequences.name: Gas detector activation and platform alarm
Emergency muster and evacuation procedures activation

controls.name: Simple Isolation Certificate Process
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify isolation points before work

supporting_text: Platform personnel did complete a Simple Isolation Certificate prior to the work being done. This entailed of personnel going over drawings and identifying isolation points as well as designated personnel physically verifying the isolation points.
the possibility of residual fluids and/or trapped pressure in the dog leg bend above the flange was overlooked

controls.name: Gas Detector ASH 0500
controls.barrier_role: Detection of hydrocarbon release to activate alarm and alert personnel
controls.lod_basis: Automated gas detection system near headers

supporting_text: The release activated a nearby gas detector at 0933 hours, sounding the platform alarm for all personnel to report to their muster station.
At 0934 hours, the Equinor Control Room Operator (CRO) radioed that gas detector ASH 0500 located near the Headers had been tripped.

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment - Safety Glasses and Face Shields
controls.barrier_role: Protection of workers from hydrocarbon spray exposure to face and eyes
controls.lod_basis: Standard PPE for work on pressurized systems

supporting_text: Both workers were wearing safety glasses and face shields at the time of the incident, which likely prevented the workers from being injured when the hydrocarbons released near their face.

controls.name: Eyewash and Shower Station
controls.barrier_role: Emergency decontamination of workers exposed to hydrocarbons
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response facility for chemical exposure

supporting_text: Despite the presence of a nearby Eyewash/Shower station, the workers opted to clean the hydrocarbons off their face shields and hard hats using the eyewash bottles that were within reach.

controls.name: Platform Muster and Emergency Response Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Organized personnel accountability and emergency response activation

supporting_text: sounding the platform alarm for all personnel to report to their muster station
By 0940 hours, full accountability of all personnel was reported at the muster station. The 'all clear' was given at 0941 hours

controls.name: Medical Evaluation and Clearance
controls.barrier_role: Assessment and treatment of worker exposure to hydrocarbons
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident medical response and occupational health protocol

supporting_text: the two workers showered, and reported to the sickbay. The workers were examined individually by the medic and a medical control physician who conducted an evaluation via telemedicine webcam. Both workers were cleared by the medic and physician of any injury and released back to full duty without any restrictions.

controls.name: Enhanced Isolation Review Procedure for Dead-Legs and Dog-Legs
controls.barrier_role: Identification and management of residual pressure in isolated line sections
controls.lod_basis: Corrective action implemented post-incident to prevent recurrence

supporting_text: Equinor's plan forward is they will review isolations to look for any possibility of hydrocarbon release from doglegs or dead-legs due to residual fluid static pressure remaining after lockout/tagout is completed, even if zero pressure is indicated on the gauge.

To ensure personnel safety, Equinor implemented engineering controls for residual fluids or static pressure release. Even if the gauge indicates zero pressure, it is important to review isolations for potential hydrocarbon release from dog-legs or dead-legs.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
